QF.id: XVI Code of Conduct and Investor Protection_17

question.text: Brokers must maintain records of all client transactions for:

answer.text: A minimum period as prescribed by SEBI — typically 5-8 years
Only until the next financial year

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI requires brokers to maintain transaction records for a prescribed minimum period (typically 5-8 years) for regulatory, audit, and dispute resolution purposes. **The Trap:** Not 1 month (too short). Record-keeping IS required. Not just until next year. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series III-A covers record retention requirements. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** As a client, maintain your own trading records independently — don't rely solely on the broker. Digital copies of contract notes, statements, and margin reports are essential.
